AAPL.reasoning: CATALYST WINDOW: Q2 FY2026 earnings fired today (April 30, 2026) — now 1 trading day post-catalyst. AAPL gap-ran from ~$271 to $285+ on the earnings result, implying a strong beat. Per the strategy rules, this qualifies as 'within 1-3 days after a clearly positive material headline that the market has not fully priced.' Analyst average target is $299-305 (~6-8% upside from current $282), with BofA at $325 and Wedbush at $350 — post-earnings drift toward those targets is the thesis. Next identifiable catalyst: iOS 27/WWDC June 2026 (~30 trading days away) — no catalyst within 10 trading days beyond the residual post-earnings momentum window. NET NEWS SENTIMENT: positive. India production ramp (1.5M iPhones airlifted) credible under the 90-day tariff truce, management guided Q2 revenue growth 13-16% YoY, Google/Gemini AI deal and Services expansion (Apple TV on Amazon Prime) are constructive. Tariff binary risk (145% scenario, margin collapse to 2-3%) remains the bear tail but is mitigated by the truce. Siri 2.0 delay to iOS 27 is a known negative already priced in. INSIDER ACTIVITY: net selling, strongly negative — CEO Tim Cook sold $12M+ at $251-256 in early April, net insider flow -$235M, zero buys. Insiders distributed $15-20 below current price. 10b5-1 plan caveat applies; treated as a distribution signal and confidence limiter. TECHNICAL SETUP GOING IN: constructive but extended post-catalyst. Daily: price above all EMAs/SMAs in aligned uptrend, RSI 61-64 (healthy, not overbought), MACD positive histogram ($0.48), volume 2.08x 20-day average (strong confirmation), OBV uptrend with no divergence. Bullish RSI divergence flagged on 60-bar lookback. Hourly: price $282 is 3.3-4% above 24-period EMAs, Bollinger %B 1.28 (above upper band — extended), hourly z-score 2.52 (stretched), RSI 67.84 (elevated). The hourly extension warrants a pullback entry rather than chasing the gap. Entry at $278-279 targets a retest of the daily upper Bollinger band ($277.97) and hourly Fib 61.8% ($273.53) as support. Target sized to 5-8% post-earnings analyst re-rating drift ($293-295 range, near Fib ext 1.272 at $293.23 and below avg analyst target $299). Stop below pre-earnings daily resistance-turned-support at $270 (prior pivot resistance at $271, now support). LESSONS INTEGRATION: recent NVDA loss triggered at intraday Fib 61.8% on stretched RSI — this trade avoids chasing the extension by waiting for pullback entry, reducing that failure-mode overlap. Confidence capped at 58 due to: (1) heavy insider net selling, (2) hourly extension suggesting short-term pullback likely before next leg, (3) no imminent catalyst within 10 trading days to drive a second binary move.
